results.title: Step-by-Step Guide to Using Gemini 3.6 Flash (+ Review ...
results.content:
On July 21, 2026, Google released three models: Gemini 3.6 Flash — the updated core model, 3.5 Flash-Lite — the fastest in the series, and 3.5 Flash Cyber — a closed cybersecurity model.
 3.6 Flash improved coding, agentic, and multimodal tasks: 49% on DeepSWE versus 37% for its predecessor, and 83% on the OSWorld-Verified computer control benchmark. The knowledge base was updated from January 2025 to March 2026.
 The model has become more efficient: it uses 17% fewer output tokens, and the API generation price has dropped from $9 to $7.5 per 1 million tokens.
 3.6 Flash is already available in the Gemini app via the model dropdown, as well as in the Gemini API, AI Studio, Android Studio, Antigravity, and Gemini Enterprise. [...] On July 21, 2026, Google unveiled three new AI models at once: Gemini 3.6 Flash, Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber. The first replaces 3.5 Flash as the ecosystem’s main “workhorse,” the second is the fastest in the lineup, and the third is a specialized model for finding and fixing vulnerabilities in code, available only through a closed program.

> We’re rolling out three new models to make AI agents faster, smarter, and cheaper at scale:  
>   
> 🔵 Gemini 3.6 Flash: It uses fewer tokens than 3.5 Flash to deliver higher quality work at the exact same cost.  
>   
> 🔵 Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite: A fast, cost-effective option for everyday tasks… pic.twitter.com/SP1eU3FCW5
>
> — Google DeepMind (@GoogleDeepMind) July 21, 2026 [...] — 83% versus 78.4%, and in office tasks GDPval-AA v2 — 1,421 points versus 1,349. The knowledge base has been updated through March 2026 — the previous version ended in January 2025.

A separate focus is efficiency. Google has taken developers’ complaints about models being “too verbose” into account: 3.6 Flash uses 17% fewer output tokens for the same tasks and requires fewer intermediate steps in multi-stage workflows. API pricing has also dropped: $1.5 per 1 million input tokens and $7.5 per 1 million output tokens — versus $9 for generation with 3.5 Flash.

The model is already available in the Gemini API, Google AI Studio, Android Studio, the Antigravity agent environment, Gemini Enterprise, and the consumer Gemini app — both on the web and on smartphones.

results.title: Introducing Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and 3.5 Flash Cyber
results.content:
3.5 Flash-Lite is the fastest model in the 3.5 series. As measured by Artificial Analysis, it runs at 350 output tokens/s. Priced at $0.3/1M input tokens and $2.5/1M output tokens and with significantly better quality than 3.1 Flash-Lite, 3.5 Flash-Lite offers a strong price-to-performance ratio for developers and customers running high throughput production traffic.

Video 5

3.5 Flash-Lite executes high volume tasks at a lower latency than 3.5 Flash. [...] 3.6 Flash: Our workhorse model that delivers better coding, knowledge work, and multimodal performance. According to the Artificial Analysis Index, it reduces output token usage by 17% compared to 3.5 Flash, and in some benchmarks like DeepSWE by Datacurve, we observe up to 65%, all at a lower cost per output token.
   3.5 Flash-Lite: Our fastest, most cost-effective 3.5-class model, delivering 350 output tokens per second according to the Artificial Analysis Index, also significantly outperforming prior Flash-Lite generations in agentic workflows. [...] Gemini 3.6 Flash builds directly on developer and customer feedback from 3.5 Flash. 3.6 Flash not only delivers a step up in coding and knowledge work, but it does this while meaningfully improving token efficiency. For example, on the Artificial Analysis Index, we see 3.6 Flash consuming 17% fewer output tokens than 3.5 Flash. It also takes fewer reasoning steps and tool calls to accomplish multi-step workflows.

This enhanced efficiency is also combined with a lower price than 3.5 Flash. At $1.50/1M input tokens and $7.50/1M output tokens, 3.6 Flash reduces the overall cost per agentic task, making agents more cost-effective to build and run.

Video 1

3.6 Flash shows better token efficiency and reduced verbosity than 3.5 Flash in an OSWorld verified task (API)

# Google Releases New Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and 3.5 Flash Cyber Models

 Laurent Giret
 Jul 21, 2026
 2

Google is making three new Gemini AI models available today to make up for the delayed release of its Gemini 3.5 Pro model, which was announced at its I/O conference in May. The headliner is the Gemini 3.6 Flash model, but there’s also the fastest and more cost-effective 3.5 Flash-Lite and the cybersecurity-focused 3.5 Flash Cyber.

According to Google, Gemini 3.6 Flash is more efficient and cheaper to use than 3.5 Flash for agentic tasks. The new model also comes with additional safeguards in the domains of Chemical, Biological, Radiological, and Nuclear (CBRN) and cyber offense misuses. [...] Lastly, Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber is Google’s response to the latest flagship cybersecurity models from rivals like OpenAI and Anthropic. Google said that it can find and fix cybersecurity vulnerabilities at “a lower price per token than larger models.”

At the moment, access to Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber is restricted to governments and trusted partners via a limited-access pilot program. Gemini 3.6 Flash and 3.5 Flash-Lite, however, are rolling out to the Gemini app, Google Search,  Google AI Studio, Android Studio, and the Gemini Enterprise app.
